generated.note: 2026-06-08 (11:02Z) targeted REFRESH after the big-site + cleanup wave on branch 1.2.1 (commits cd9fa64, 0c0ed42, 2b8c4a2, 047e6a9, a1b8ded; last manifest refresh was 4894736). Two static-analysis findings CLEARED this wave: (1) grid_track_overflow_risks 51->0 — every bare 1fr grid track across 18 CSS files was wrapped to minmax(0,1fr); ground-truth grep for a bare 1fr in a grid-template declaration outside minmax() now returns 0 (auto-fill/auto-fit repeat() tracks like minmax(160px,1fr) are intentional and never were overflow risks). (2) rest_hang_risks 68->11 — the 57 block view.js fetch() sites were the resolved set: all 9 data-fetching block view.js now `import { wcbFetch } from '@wcb/fetch'` and call wcbFetch() instead of native fetch(); wcbFetch (assets/js/modules/wcb-fetch.js, registered as the @wcb/fetch script module in core/class-plugin.php via wp_register_script_module on init, declared as a dep in each block's view.asset.php) wraps fetch with a 15000ms AbortController timeout that composes any caller signal. The 11 REMAINING rest_hang_risks are all admin-side and OUT OF SCOPE of this wave: assets/js/admin.js (5 wp.apiFetch), assets/js/wizard.js (3 wp.apiFetch), assets/js/admin/application-detail.js (1 native fetch), assets/js/admin/emails.js (2 native fetch) — none route through wcbFetch yet.
